pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Old Javanese / Kawi tattva corpus (San Hyan series, Vrhaspatitattva, Gaṇapatitattva, Slokantara, Vratisasana, Kunjarakarna, Kalpabuddha)
series_anchor.palette: tropical earth ochre, deep palm-leaf brown, weathered teak, jasmine cream, faint lapis trace
series_anchor.mood: Old Javanese palm-leaf manuscript tradition. Tropical pre-dawn humidity. Frangipani at the threshold. The text inscribed with an iron stylus.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Earth ochre and palm-brown washed wet-on-wet to simulate the patina of palm-leaf age. Tropical greenery suggested only as a faint background tone, never literally rendered. Lapis used as a single bright accent on the subject only if the work demands it.

1_context.content_excerpt:
### Paragraph 1

This is the royal command of the Lord, the divine Buddha, this great instruction must be heeded, listen to all the `mantra`s. Lord Buddha was in the great dissolution (`mahāpralaya`), his body came into being, he then became the Lord Pañcatathāgata. Their bodies and names are distinct, each has a counterpart in form. His `mudrā` is the `bodhyagrī`, his `jñāna` is `śāśvata`, his implement is the `cakra`. Sahāvatī is the name of his dominion, a lion is his mount. She, Lady Sattvavajrī, she, Lady Dharmavajrī, she, Lady Karmavajrī, they are the `devī`s accompanying him. Now, as fo

output.reasoning: The Kalpabuddha opens with the Pañcatathāgata's cosmic emergence and the cakra as a named implement of divine jñāna — a single ritual wheel evokes the dissolution-and-becoming at the text's heart without illustrating any figure or scene. The cakra also carries the weight of the Kawi tattva corpus's meditative cosmology.
output.subject: A single stone dharma wheel, worn and mossy at its rim
output.palette: tropical earth ochre, palm-leaf brown, faint lapis trace
output.composition: The wheel sits low and left of center, roughly one-third into the picture plane, tilted very slightly as if resting on an unseen surface. The upper right two-thirds of the composition is open warm cream paper, breathing and unoccupied. The visual weight of the stone mass anchors the lower left without geometric symmetry.
output.watercolor_treatment: Earth ochre and palm-brown laid wet-on-wet, pooling darker pigment along the carved spoke recesses and diluting outward to near-nothing at the rim's edge. Cold-press grain shows through the thin outer wash. A single restrained touch of faint lapis in one spoke hollow as the sole accent. Dry-brush scuffs describe the mossy, pitted stone texture at the top of the rim where the brush ran nearly dry. No hard outlines — all edges bleed softly into the cream paper.
output.prompt: A single ancient stone dharma wheel, worn and mossy at its carved rim, rests low and left of center on warm cream cotton paper, tilted slightly as though set down in a moment of stillness. Earth ochre and palm-leaf brown wash wet-on-wet across the stone mass, pooling darker pigment deep in the spoke recesses and dissolving outward until the rim's edge nearly vanishes into the paper. Cold-press grain shows through the thinning wash. One spoke hollow holds a single restrained touch of faint lapis — the sole accent. Dry-brush scuffs across the upper rim describe pitted, age-worn stone. All edges bleed softly, never outlined. The upper right of the composition is open, unoccupied, breathing — more than half the painting is warm cream silence.

output.reasoning: Only material issue is weak hedging in the physical placement; no gate-failing AI-slop, namedropping, watercolor-labeling, palette drift, or mood drift is present.

The critic flags only one medium-severity issue: 'tilted slightly' is weak hedging. The suggestion 'tilted with a shallow diagonal lean' is more precise and declarative without introducing any new ideas or conflicting with the series anchor. Accept it.

decisions.reason: 'Tilted with a shallow diagonal lean' is more precise and declarative than 'tilted slightly,' eliminating weak hedging without changing the composition intent.

output.final_prompt: A single ancient stone dharma wheel, worn and mossy at its carved rim, rests low and left of center on warm cream cotton paper, tilted with a shallow diagonal lean as though set down in a moment of stillness. Earth ochre and palm-leaf brown wash wet-on-wet across the stone mass, pooling darker pigment deep in the spoke recesses and dissolving outward until the rim's edge nearly vanishes into the paper. Cold-press grain shows through the thinning wash. One spoke hollow holds a single restrained touch of faint lapis — the sole accent. Dry-brush scuffs across the upper rim describe pitted, age-worn stone. All edges bleed softly, never outlined. The upper right of the composition is open, unoccupied, breathing — more than half the painting is warm cream silence.
output.changes_summary: Replaced 'tilted slightly' with 'tilted with a shallow diagonal lean' to remove weak hedging and sharpen the physical placement description. No other changes were made.

final_prompt: A single ancient stone dharma wheel, worn and mossy at its carved rim, rests low and left of center on warm cream cotton paper, tilted with a shallow diagonal lean as though set down in a moment of stillness. Earth ochre and palm-leaf brown wash wet-on-wet across the stone mass, pooling darker pigment deep in the spoke recesses and dissolving outward until the rim's edge nearly vanishes into the paper. Cold-press grain shows through the thinning wash. One spoke hollow holds a single restrained touch of faint lapis — the sole accent. Dry-brush scuffs across the upper rim describe pitted, age-worn stone. All edges bleed softly, never outlined. The upper right of the composition is open, unoccupied, breathing — more than half the painting is warm cream silence.
